claim: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.

claim: Senior leadership establishes a board-sanctioned cybersecurity framework.

claim: Internal control effectiveness is verified by an independent assessor.

claim: Vendor relationships require continuous risk reassessment.

claim: Recovery plans undergo yearly exercise validation.

claim: High-value transactions trigger stronger identity verification.

claim: Encryption is required when financial data crosses network boundaries.

claim: Cyber events demand prompt notification to relevant regulators.

claim: Management may delegate the entire security program to outside counsel.
source_clauses: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.

claim: Self-audit is sufficient to validate internal controls.

Internal audit reports to the audit committee, not management.

claim: Vendor due diligence is completed once at contract signing only.

Initial due diligence is necessary but not sufficient.

claim: Business continuity plans should remain confidential and untested.

claim: Single-factor authentication is acceptable for wire transfers above $1M.

claim: Financial data may be transmitted in cleartext within the corporate network.

claim: Cyber incidents may be reported when convenient at quarter-end.

claim: FFIEC publishes a quarterly art catalog featuring board members.

claim: Removable media must be managed per documented procedures.

claim: Supplier relationships shall include information security requirements.

claim: Departmental security goals are formally defined and aligned with policy.

claim: Every unacceptable risk has a corresponding remediation roadmap.

claim: Background checks are completed before staff begin employment.

claim: Portable storage media handling is governed by formal procedures.

claim: Vendor contracts spell out cybersecurity obligations.

claim: Cryptographic algorithms are reviewed for adequacy at planned intervals.

claim: Continuous monitoring evaluates the security management system performance.

claim: Information security objectives are optional for non-regulated functions.
source_clauses: Information security objectives must be established at relevant functions and levels.
All functions are in scope of the management system.

claim: Risks deemed unacceptable can be ignored if mitigation costs exceed budget.
source_clauses: Risk treatment plans must address all unacceptable risks.
Cost-benefit analysis informs but does not override risk acceptance.

claim: Personnel screening can be deferred until probation completion.
source_clauses: Personnel screening shall be conducted prior to employment.

claim: Removable media policies do not apply to executive officers.
source_clauses: Removable media must be managed per documented procedures.
Applicability of controls is universal across the organization.

claim: Supplier security requirements apply only to direct contracts.
source_clauses: Supplier relationships shall include information security requirements.

claim: Cryptographic controls are configured once and remain effective indefinitely.
source_clauses: Cryptographic controls shall be reviewed for ongoing effectiveness.
Cryptographic agility prepares for algorithm transitions.

claim: Performance evaluation is a one-time activity at certification.
source_clauses: Performance evaluation is conducted through monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation.
Evaluation is continuous, not a single event.

claim: ISO 27001 requires organizations to host an annual costume contest.
source_clauses: Information security objectives must be established at relevant functions and levels.
Risk treatment plans must address all unacceptable risks.

claim: The standard mandates a chief mascot officer reporting to the CISO.
source_clauses: Personnel screening shall be conducted prior to employment.
Supplier relationships shall include information security requirements.

claim: Annex A requires a poetry recitation at every internal audit closing meeting.
source_clauses: Performance evaluation is conducted through monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation.
Cryptographic controls shall be reviewed for ongoing effectiveness.

claim: Removable media must be color-coded purple to denote sensitivity.
source_clauses: Removable media must be managed per documented procedures.
Risk treatment plans must address all unacceptable risks.

claim: Certification renewal includes a mandatory tea ceremony with the auditor.
source_clauses: Information security objectives must be established at relevant functions and levels.
Performance evaluation is conducted through monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation.

claim: Monthly vulnerability scans must include operating system, web application, and database layers.
source_clauses: Monthly vulnerability scans must include operating system, web application, and database layers.
Scan results are submitted to the AO alongside the monthly POA&M update.

claim: High-severity vulnerabilities must be remediated within 30 days of discovery.
source_clauses: High-severity vulnerabilities must be remediated within 30 days of discovery.
Critical vulnerabilities require remediation within 15 days.

claim: The annual security assessment must be performed by an accredited 3PAO.
source_clauses: The annual security assessment must be performed by an accredited 3PAO.
Assessment results feed into the continuous monitoring strategy.

claim: Continuous monitoring strategy documents must specify metrics for each control family.
source_clauses: Continuous monitoring strategy documents must specify metrics for each control family.
The strategy addresses frequency of assessment per control volatility.

claim: POA&M items must include estimated completion dates and responsible parties.
source_clauses: POA&M items must include estimated completion dates and responsible parties.
Milestones track incremental progress toward full remediation.

claim: Overdue POA&M items require documented justification for schedule extension.
source_clauses: Overdue POA&M items require documented justification for schedule extension.
Extensions beyond 90 days must be approved by the authorizing official.

claim: Vulnerability scans need to cover the full technology stack on a monthly basis.
source_clauses: Monthly vulnerability scans must include operating system, web application, and database layers.
Automated scanning tools must be configured to scan all system components.

claim: Findings classified as critical must be fixed within two weeks.
source_clauses: Critical vulnerabilities require remediation within 15 days.
Severity classification follows CVSS v3.1 scoring methodology.

claim: Organizations should define how often they assess each control based on how frequently it changes.
source_clauses: The continuous monitoring strategy addresses frequency of assessment per control volatility.
Higher-volatility controls warrant more frequent assessment cycles.

claim: Each remediation plan entry tracks who is responsible and when they expect to finish.
source_clauses: POA&M items must include estimated completion dates and responsible parties.
The POA&M template requires milestone-level tracking of progress.

claim: Remediation milestones provide checkpoints toward resolving the overall weakness.
source_clauses: Milestones track incremental progress toward full remediation.
Each milestone defines a measurable outcome contributing to closure.

claim: The yearly comprehensive assessment produces an updated system security plan and assessment report.
source_clauses: The annual security assessment produces a refreshed Security Assessment Report.
Updated SSP sections are submitted within 30 days of assessment completion.

claim: Quarterly penetration testing is mandated for all FedRAMP High systems.
source_clauses: Monthly vulnerability scans must include operating system, web application, and database layers.
Annual penetration testing validates the effectiveness of boundary protections.

claim: POA&M items are automatically closed after 90 days if no activity is recorded.
source_clauses: Overdue POA&M items require documented justification for schedule extension.
Extensions beyond 90 days must be approved by the authorizing official.

claim: Continuous monitoring replaces the need for periodic security assessments.
source_clauses: Continuous monitoring supplements periodic security assessments.
The monitoring strategy does not eliminate the need for formal assessment events.

claim: All vulnerabilities regardless of severity must be fixed within 30 days.
source_clauses: High-severity vulnerabilities must be remediated within 30 days of discovery.
Low-severity findings may be addressed within 180 days per the ConMon guidance.

claim: The authorizing official must approve every individual POA&M milestone.
source_clauses: Extensions beyond 90 days must be approved by the authorizing official.
Day-to-day milestone tracking is managed by the system owner.

claim: Metrics for continuous monitoring are defined at the system level only.
source_clauses: Continuous monitoring strategy documents must specify metrics for each control family.
Metrics cascade from organizational to system-specific implementation tiers.

claim: FedRAMP requires daily vulnerability scans of all production endpoints.
source_clauses: Monthly vulnerability scans must include operating system, web application, and database layers.
Scan frequency is monthly per the ConMon Strategy Guide.

claim: POA&M items for inherited controls are the responsibility of the tenant, not the CSP.
source_clauses: Inherited controls are the responsibility of the underlying cloud service provider.
Tenant-specific POA&M items address only hybrid and system-specific controls.

claim: NIST 800-53 CA-7 mandates real-time automated remediation of all detected vulnerabilities.
source_clauses: CA-7 requires ongoing assessment of security controls at a frequency sufficient to support risk-based decisions.
Automated tools support monitoring but do not mandate automated remediation.

claim: The 3PAO assessment can be performed by internal audit staff if they hold CISSP certification.
source_clauses: The annual security assessment must be performed by an accredited 3PAO.
3PAO accreditation is granted by A2LA or an equivalent recognized body.

claim: Completed POA&M items are permanently deleted from the system after verification.
source_clauses: Completed POA&M items transition to a closed state with documented evidence of remediation.
Historical POA&M records must be retained for the duration of the authorization.

claim: Continuous monitoring data must be stored exclusively in FedRAMP-authorized cloud storage.
source_clauses: Monitoring data storage must comply with the system's data handling requirements.
Storage location is determined by the system security categorization and boundary definition.
